items.title: Eredivisie is dit seizoen Europees kampioen nederlagen lijden
items.summary: Geen enkel land lijdt dit Europese seizoen zoveel nederlagen als Nederland. Na de Champions League-wedstrijden van woensdag staat het aantal verliesbeurten op dertig.

items.title: VI Live: 'Antony speelt maximaal een helft', Braga-fans vermaken zich in Deventer
items.summary: Welkom in de VI Live van donderdag 29 januari. Het is de dag van de beslissingen in de eerste fase van de Europa League. Mis hier niets van Real Betis - Feyenoord, Go Ahead Eagles - Sporting Braga en Celtic - FC Utrecht.

items.title: Brentford latest: Nelson loan not ideal but Andrews backs Arsenal man to star
USWNT legend Dunn announces retirement at 33
items.summary: United States women's national team great Crystal Dunn on Thursday announced her retirement from soccer at age 33.

items.title: Pep: City panicked over Mourinho's UCL strategy
items.summary: Pep Guardiola revealed Manchester City were panicking over their Champions League hopes as Benfica pushed for a late goal against Real Madrid.

items.title: Napoli: Sterling 'financial demands' block move
items.summary: Napoli sporting director Giovanni Manna has said Raheem Sterling's "significant financial demands" could be an obstacle in doing a deal for the forward.

items.title: Officieel: Pasveer verlaat Ajax en keert terug op het oude nest
items.summary: Remko Pasveer is teruggekeerd op het oude nest. De 42-jarige doelman komt over van Ajax en moet Heracles Almelo redden in de strijd om lijfsbehoud.

items.title: Waarom Ajax Pasveer zal missen
items.summary: Remko Pasveer verruilt Ajax voor Heracles Almelo . In de aflevering van Rondje Europa van donderdag besprak Ajax-watcher Lentin Goodijk de toen nog nakende transfer.

items.title: Arsenal in talks to sign Barcelona's Batlle in summer
items.summary: Arsenal have held advanced discussions with Barcelona full-back Ona Batlle in the hope of reaching a pre-contract agreement to sign her in the summer.

items.title: Wintertransfers Eredivisie seizoen 2025/26: alle clubs op een rij
items.summary: De transferperiode is weer losgebarsten. In dit overzicht van VI vind je alle afgeronde, door clubs bevestigde, wintertransfers van de achttien Eredivisie-clubs. Clubs in Nederland kunnen tot en met 3 februari spelers aantrekken .

items.title: BBC will not fund pundits working for rival podcasts during World Cup
items.summary: Licence fee payers’ money will not be used this summer Micah Richards will be on The Rest is Football podcast The BBC has made clear it will not use licence fee payers’ money to help leading pundits such as Micah Richards to work for rival podcasts during this summer’s World Cup. Cost constraints and environmental considerations mean the BBC’s World Cup presentation will come from its Salford studios for the bulk of the tournament, with Richards expected to have a leading role. However, he has also committed to appearing on The Rest is Football podcast, which will be presented by Gary Lineker in New York. Continue reading...

items.title: Thirteen clubs punished for match-fixing in China
items.summary: Nine teams will start the upcoming Chinese Super League season on minus points after clubs were sanctioned following an investigation.

items.title: Sterling’s time at Chelsea was not fruitful but he still has time to revive career
items.summary: The forward seems to have lost a yard of pace but he is only 31 and leaving Stamford Bridge to make a fresh start may be the best thing for him While Raheem Sterling’s bank balance was boosted by his unhappy spell at Chelsea, the professional cost has been huge. The forward’s career has nosedived since his departure from Manchester City three and a half years ago. Sterling was hailed as a marquee signing when he joined Chelsea in the summer of 2022 but there was no place for him inside the tent by the time an agreement was finally reached to end his £325,000-a-week contract by mutual consent on Wednesday. The decline has been sad to watch. There was excitement when Sterling became the first player to join Chelsea after the Todd Boehly-Clearlake Capital takeover. He had won four Premier League titles with City and had undoubted pedigree. Thomas Tuchel wanted his threat in the final third and much was made of Sterling, who grew up near Wembley, returning to London when Chelsea signed him for £47.5m. Continue reading...

items.title: Premier League withdrew Ryan Giggs’s Hall of Fame invitation amid court case
items.summary: Giggs was invited to be inaugural member in 2020 Talks ongoing to include former Manchester United star Ryan Giggs was invited to become an inaugural member of the Premier League Hall of Fame in 2020, only for the offer to be withdrawn. Manchester United’s record appearance-maker was chosen alongside Alan Shearer to become the first inductees to the Hall of Fame in 2020, only for the event to be postponed because of the Covid pandemic. At a rescheduled event in 2021, Giggs was replaced by Thierry Henry. Continue reading...

items.title: Raducanu splits from coach Roig after just six months
items.summary: Emma Raducanu has split from coach Francis Roig after just six months working with the Spaniard.

items.title: Football Daily | ‘The big guy’ lights up another chaotic European night and saves Mourinho
items.summary: Sign up now! Sign up now! Sign up now? Sign up now! Football fans are used to seeing a goalkeeper charge upfield for a set piece in the last few minutes of a game. Jan Tomaszewski, the Polish “clown” who denied England a place at the 1974 World Cup, may have been the first to do so, and it has become normalised in the 21st century. On Wednesday in Lisbon, there was a new twist on an old tactic. In the 98th minute, Benfica keeper Anatoliy Trubin legged it upfield – at a time when his team were leading Real Madrid. Continue reading...

items.title: Champions League recap: Grading all 36 teams, plus...
items.summary: The league phase of the Champions League is over, culminating with a dramatic Matchday 8. Who was the best and worst in this stage of the competition?

items.title: Talking points: Mourinho's master class, biggest d...
items.summary: ESPN FC's writers pick out their highs and lows of the tournament to this point.

items.title: Palhinha ready for the real Spurs to stand up after Champions League stroll
items.summary: Tottenham eased into the last 16 and the midfielder trusts their uplift will provide ‘a big jump’ in the Premier League It was a question most assuredly in keeping with the overall craziness of the situation. Tottenham: 14th in the Premier League, with two wins out of 14 in the competition, some of their fans beginning to fret about relegation. Also out of both domestic cups. Tottenham: the fourth best team in Europe after the conclusion of the league phase of the Champions League. On a fast-track to the last 16. So, of course, it was put to João Palhinha as he left the stadium after Spurs’s convincing 2-0 win at Eintracht Frankfurt on Wednesday night. Can Spurs win the Champions League? The midfielder’s response was to chuckle. And then laugh a little more. “I know what you want to hear from me,” he said. Continue reading...

items.title: Palace agree £50m Strand Larsen deal as Forest chase Mateta
items.summary: Crystal Palace agree a deal in principle worth in the region of £50m to sign Wolves striker Jorgen Strand Larsen.
